english: Spiced Yellow Chicken Soup with Rice Vermicelli

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Beehoon Soto; A0FKP (noodle soup) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a rice-vermicelli soup dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Beehoon Soto or equivalent noodle soup dish in the clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Beehoon Soto as typically served at a Singapore hawker centre is approximately 400 g, comprising rice vermicelli, chicken pieces, bean sprouts, hard-boiled egg, and spiced yellow coconut-chicken broth.

nutrient_source.title: Beehoon Soto — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Beehoon Soto (cr_id F05060016). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice vermicelli / rice noodle analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beehoon Soto as a composite dish. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice vermicelli / rice noodles (GI range 58–70, representative value 65), which is the dominant carbohydrate-contributing ingredient. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 13.72) / 100 ≈ 8.9, rounded to 9.1 accounting for minor additional carbohydrate from bean sprouts and egg. Serving carbohydrate = 3.43 g/100g × 400 g serving = 13.72 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 170 mg/100 g; approximately 680 mg per 400 g bowl
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content of 170 mg/100 g is below the 200 mg/100 g low-sodium threshold per 100 g, but a full 400 g bowl delivers approximately 680 mg sodium — about 30% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — so patients with hypertension should avoid adding high-sodium condiments such as sambal or kecap manis and request reduced-salt broth preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rice vermicelli is a refined high-GI carbohydrate (GI ~65); dietary fibre at 0.39 g/100 g provides negligible glycaemic buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should request a reduced beehoon portion and increased bean sprout and vegetable content to lower the glycaemic load, or substitute glass noodles (tang hoon) for a lower-GI alternative, and avoid sweet table condiments such as kecap manis.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 1.92 g/100 g; estimated saturated fat ~0.95 g/100 g from coconut milk fraction; sodium 170 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Beehoon Soto is a low-calorie, low-fat soup dish suitable for cardiovascular disease patients in moderate portions; however, patients should request reduced coconut milk preparation to minimise saturated fat intake and avoid adding fried shallots or oily condiments at the table.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coconut milk saturated fat and refined rice vermicelli (pro-inflammatory refined carbohydrate); turmeric provides curcumin anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with Rheumatoid Arthritis should request reduced coconut milk to lower saturated fat, request extra turmeric in the spice paste for curcumin benefit, and increase bean sprout and vegetable content to improve anti-inflammatory fibre intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beehoon Soto is a traditional Singaporean-Malay hawker dish consisting of thin rice vermicelli noodles (beehoon) served in a fragrant, spiced yellow chicken broth. The soup base is prepared by simmering chicken with a spice paste of turmeric, lemongrass, galangal, and shallots, often enriched with coconut milk to produce a mildly creamy, golden-yellow broth. The dish is typically garnished with shredded chicken, bean sprouts, a hard-boiled egg, fried shallots, and fresh celery leaves. Beehoon Soto is a staple at Singapore hawker centres and coffee shops, particularly popular as a breakfast or lunch option. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie dish at 40 kcal/100 g due to its high water content as a soup, with moderate protein (2.19 g/100 g) from chicken, low fat (1.92 g/100 g), and relatively low carbohydrate (3.43 g/100 g) per 100 g. However, the rice vermicelli component is a refined high-GI carbohydrate (GI ~65), and the dish provides minimal dietary fibre (0.39 g/100 g), which limits glycaemic buffering. Sodium at 170 mg/100 g is moderate; a full bowl (approximately 400 g) delivers around 680 mg sodium. The turmeric in the spice paste contributes curcumin, which has documented anti-inflammatory properties. The dish is halal and gluten-free. Clinically, it is suitable for hypertension patients in moderate portions without added condiments, requires portion management for Type 2 Diabetes due to the high-GI noodle component, and is acceptable for cardiovascular disease patients when coconut milk is minimised.
common_user_queries: Is beehoon soto suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05060016) at confidence 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using rice vermicelli as the analogous food, which is the dominant carbohydrate source in the dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Beehoon Soto are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F05060016) as of 2026. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.95 g/100 g based on coconut milk fraction contribution in the diluted soup; this is an internal estimate as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using rice vermicelli/rice noodles as the reference food. Input cultural tags 'western' and 'keto' corrected: Beehoon Soto is a Singaporean-Malay dish and is not keto-compatible due to its rice vermicelli carbohydrate content. Input primary_cuisine 'western' corrected to 'Singaporean-Malay'. Rheumatoid arthritis health context preserved from input as an additional condition.
